lessons.title: Noting story grammar - Part 1
lessons.passageText:
Mang Berto is a factory worker. He works in a candy factory in Laguna. He wakes up early and walks every morning to go to work. How hard Mang Berto works in the factory!

Then, he walks back to his house to eat dinner with his family. Why does he walk every day? Mang Berto wants to save money for his family. What a great man! Do you also save money like Mang Berto?

questions.questionText: Who is the main character in the first story?
Where does Mang Berto work?
What challenge does Mang Berto face every day?

choices.choiceText: He wants to save money for his family
He is always late for work
He is too tired to work

questions.questionText: Arrange Mang Berto’s day events in order:

choices.choiceText: He walks every morning to go to work.
He works hard in the candy factory.
He walks back home after work.
He eats dinner with his family.

questions.questionText: How does Mang Berto solve his problem of saving money?

lessons.title: Noting story grammar - Part 2
lessons.passageText:
At the school grounds

Jo and his friends were playing baseball at the school grounds. They were having so much fun. When the ball was hit, it flew into the air, and Jo ran to catch it.

Instead of catching the ball, it hit his head. He fell to the ground. "My head really hurts," he told his teacher. His teacher brought him to the school nurse. She gave him an ice pack for his head. Soon, his head began to feel better.

questions.questionText: Who are the characters in the second story?

choices.choiceText: Jo, his friends, his teacher, and the school nurse.

questions.questionText: Where did the story happen?
What problem did Jo face while playing baseball?

choices.choiceText: The ball hit Jo’s head, and it hurt.
He got into a fight with his friends.

questions.questionText: Arrange what happened first, next, and last in the story:

choices.choiceText: First: Jo and his friends played baseball.
Next: The ball hit Jo’s head, and he told his teacher.
Last: The nurse gave him an ice pack, and he felt better.

questions.questionText: How was Jo’s problem solved?

choices.choiceText: The school nurse gave him an ice pack, and his head began to feel better.
His friends took him to the hospital.

lessons.title: Author’s POV
lessons.passageText:
Point of View means who is telling the story.

First Person
The storyteller is inside the story. Uses words like I, me, we. Example: I walked to school.

Second Person
The storyteller is talking to you. Uses words like you, your. Example: You wake up early.

Third Person
The storyteller is outside the story. Uses words like he, she, they. Example: Jo played baseball with his friends.

Third Person Omniscient (All-Knowing)
The storyteller knows everything about all the characters. Can tell what everyone is thinking and feeling. Example: Jo was excited to play, but his teacher worried he might get hurt.

questions.questionText:
My biggest dream in life is to become a doctor. I love watching movies where doctors are happy about saving the lives of people. I feel amazed at how doctors are being respected in our community. I want to become a doctor because I want to help my dearest grandfather fight cancer.
What POV is used?

You walk into your classroom early in the morning. You place your bag on the desk and greet your classmates with a smile. When the bell rings, you stand up and join the flag ceremony. You sing “Lupang Hinirang” with pride.
What POV is used?

Mrs. Reyes is the best teacher. She always makes sure our classroom looks good. Sometimes she rewards us with chocolates. And she is always smiling whenever she talks to us. She is the best and we love her.
What POV is used?

Joseph and his friends went to Manila Zoo. They had fun watching the different animals. Also, they learned many things about these animals. Joseph even made a list of animals and took pictures in the zoo. He was also amazed to see many people visiting the place. When Joseph arrived home, he told his family about their trip to Manila Zoo.
What POV is used?

Nida is a helpful child. She always helps her mother in cooking and cleaning the house. Sometimes she helps his father in the store during her free time. She always feels happy lending her helping hands.
What POV is used?

lessons.title: Sequencing Events
lessons.passageText:
Sequencing means putting events in the correct order — what happens first, next, and last. It’s like following steps in cooking or playing a game.

Stories also have a beginning, middle, and end. If we mix them up, the story is confusing. Sequencing helps us understand the story clearly.

questions.questionText: Arrange the morning routine in correct order:
Arrange the steps for planting a seed:

choices.choiceText: You dig a small hole in the garden.
You place the seed inside the hole.
You cover the seed with soil.
You water the soil every day.
You see the seed grow into a plant.
You watch the plant grow taller.

questions.questionText: Arrange the steps of riding a jeepney:

choices.choiceText: You hand your fare to the driver.
You get off at your destination.

questions.questionText: Arrange Joseph's trip to the zoo:

choices.choiceText: Joseph and his friends went to Manila Zoo.
They had fun watching the different animals.
They learned many things about the animals.
Joseph made a list of animals and took pictures.
Joseph was amazed to see many people visiting the zoo.
Joseph told his family about their trip.

questions.questionText: Arrange the story of Cinderella:

choices.choiceText: Cinderella was humiliated by her stepmother and stepsisters.
Prince Charming invited every woman to join the ball.
Cinderella’s fairy godmother helped her attend the ball.
Cinderella danced all night with Prince Charming.
Cinderella hurriedly went home and left one glass shoe.
Prince Charming let every woman try on the shoe, and it fit Cinderella.

lessons.title: Sequential Plot
lessons.passageText:
The Real Champion
By: Syrelle France S. Paterter

Aya Shanelle is a graceful dancer. She loves to join events such as dancing competitions. There are times that she ends up being the loser of the event. But that didn’t stop her from becoming more passionate about her craft.

She continuously joins more exciting dance competitions. Win or lose, it didn’t matter. What matters for her the most are the lessons learned and friends gained in every contest. That is the true essence of being a real champion.

questions.questionText: What is Aya Shanelle’s talent?
What happened first in the story?

choices.choiceText: Aya Shanelle is introduced as a graceful dancer who loves joining competitions.
She becomes more passionate about her craft.
She values lessons learned and friends gained.

questions.questionText: What does Aya Shanelle gain from joining contests, aside from winning?
Arrange these jumbled events in the correct order:

choices.choiceText: Aya Shanelle is a graceful dancer.
She loves to join dancing competitions.
She becomes more passionate about her craft.
She continuously joins more exciting competitions.
She values lessons learned and friends gained.

questions.questionText: At the end of the story, what does Aya Shanelle realize is the true essence of being a champion?

choices.choiceText: That winning or losing doesn’t matter — what matters most are the lessons learned and the friends gained.
That she needs to practice harder.

lessons.title: Drawing Conclusions
lessons.passageText:
Buddy and the Cat

Little Buddy Cockroach is a house bug. Mother Cockroach always tells him to stay in dark places and hide. He listens to her with half an ear not believing in what she says. “How could they not like me? I have beautiful, shiny wings, and long antenna. Surely, they would love me,” he thought. One day, Buddy saw a black cat and said, “I will show Mother Cockroach that she is wrong.” But when the cat saw him, it immediately jumped and started running after him with its sharp claws. Buddy ran as fast as he could to the dark place under the cabinet and never came out.

questions.questionText: Buddy thought people would love him because of his shiny wings and long antenna. What can we conclude about Buddy’s personality?

choices.choiceText: He is proud and overconfident about his appearance.
He is very smart and humble.
He doesn't care about his looks.

questions.questionText: Mother Cockroach always told Buddy to stay in dark places and hide. What can we conclude about why she gave this advice?

choices.choiceText: She knows that cockroaches are disliked and unsafe in the open, so she wanted to protect him.
She didn't want Buddy to play outside.
She wanted all the food for herself.
She was afraid of the dark.

questions.questionText: Buddy ignored his mother’s warning and approached the cat. What can we conclude about Buddy’s attitude toward his mother’s advice?

choices.choiceText: He was careless and didn’t believe her, showing disobedience.
He was very obedient and followed instructions.
He only wanted to make friends with the cat.

questions.questionText: The cat immediately chased Buddy with sharp claws. What can we conclude about how animals or people usually react to cockroaches?

choices.choiceText: They see cockroaches as pests or threats and try to get rid of them.
They love to play with cockroaches.
They are afraid and run away from them.

questions.questionText: Buddy ran to the dark place under the cabinet and never came out. What can we conclude about what Buddy learned from this experience?

choices.choiceText: He realized his mother was right, and it’s safer to stay hidden rather than risk danger.
He decided to try again the next day.
He was angry at the cat and wanted revenge.
He found a new friend under the cabinet.

lessons.title: Identifying the main idea
lessons.passageText: The Filipino people are very fond of festivals and celebrations. The Panagbenga Festival in Baguio City is celebrated in February. People from far and near visit the place to see the parade of floats that are decorated with fragrant and multi-colored flowers. The Pampanga’s Hot Air Balloon Festival is also a fun and colorful activity. It happens yearly between January and February at the Clark Freeport Zone.

questions.questionText: The Philippines has many fruits. First, there is the mango. Second, there is the banana. Third, there is the papaya. Finally, there is the coconut. Is this passage Enumeration or Description?
The mango is sweet, yellow, and juicy when ripe. It has a smooth skin and a pleasant smell that makes people want to eat it. Is this passage Enumeration or Description?
Filipino houses often have gardens. Some grow vegetables, others plant flowers, and many keep fruit trees. Each garden adds beauty to the home. Is this passage Enumeration or Description?
The Sinigang soup tastes sour because of tamarind. It is hot, comforting, and filled with vegetables and meat. The smell is strong but delicious. Is this passage Enumeration or Description?
When is the Panagbenga Festival celebrated?
What do people see during the Panagbenga Festival parade?

choices.choiceText: Floats decorated with fragrant and multi-colored flowers.

questions.questionText: Based on the passage, what is the main idea about Filipino people and their festivals?

choices.choiceText: Filipino people love colorful festivals and celebrations, such as the Panagbenga Festival and the Hot Air Balloon Festival.
Filipino people only celebrate festivals in February.
The Hot Air Balloon Festival is the only festival in the Philippines.
The Panagbenga Festival is about hot air balloons.

lessons.title: Making a Summary - Part 1
lessons.passageText: One sunny morning at Mabuti Elementary School, Zarie was the only pupil who comes to school the earliest. She saw the canteen manager, Aling Nena, cleaning the canteen. She helped Aling Nena clean the canteen. As Zarie is sweeping the floor, she found a one hundred peso bill and gave it to Aling Nena who thanked her for the honest deed she did. As a reward, Aling Nena gave Zarie a pouch of chocolates. She thanked Aling Nena for the reward she received.

questions.questionText: Who was cleaning the canteen when Zarie arrived?
What did Zarie find while sweeping the floor?
If you put together the most important details, what is the summary of Zarie's story?

choices.choiceText: Zarie helped clean the canteen, found money, returned it honestly to Aling Nena, and was rewarded with chocolates.
Zarie arrived at school early and ate chocolates.
Zarie found money and kept it for herself.
Aling Nena cleaned the canteen alone.

lessons.title: Making a Summary - Part 2
lessons.passageText: It was Carlo’s 10th birthday. His family decorated their home with balloons and colorful streamers. On the table, there was pancit for long life, fried chicken, and a big chocolate cake with ten candles. Carlo’s cousins brought gifts wrapped in bright paper. After singing “Happy Birthday,” Carlo blew out the candles and made a wish. Everyone clapped, ate together, and played games. The celebration was filled with laughter and joy.

questions.questionText: Why do Filipinos serve pancit during birthdays?

choices.choiceText: Because Carlo likes it the most.

questions.questionText: If you put together the most important details, what is the summary of Carlo's story?

choices.choiceText: Carlo’s family celebrated his 10th birthday with food, decorations, gifts, cake, and games, showing love and joy.
Carlo blew out ten candles and made a wish.
Carlo's cousins brought gifts wrapped in bright paper.
The family ate pancit, fried chicken, and cake.

lessons.title: Fact and Opinion in a Narrative
lessons.passageText:
A fact is something that is true and supported by evidence.
An opinion is only a belief. It is also an expression of a person’s feelings. Clue words like “think,” “believe,” “feel,” “probably,” or “seems” signal an opinion.

Boys and Girls
At school, during English class, Mr. Mahinay asked the class to group themselves and make a thank you card as an output. All the groups were busy doing their output.

“Jilian, do you have another bond paper? May I ask for 2 pieces please,” asked Zaira.
“I think I have plenty. Here, take 3 pieces,” said Jilian, giving the pieces of bond paper to Zaira.
“Thank you, Jilian. You are so kind,” said Zaira.
Zarie was busy coloring the thank you card, when Zaira borrowed one of her crayons.
“May I borrow your yellow crayon, Zarie,” said Zaira.
“Here, Zaira,” said Zarie, handing over the yellow crayon to Zaira.
“I believe your card is more beautiful than mine,” said Zaira.
“Perhaps Jilian’s thank you card is the most beautiful,” replied Zarie.
“I don’t think so,” said Jilian. “I think all of our thank-you cards are beautiful.”
“Jilian is right,” declared Mr. Mahinay. “Every artwork is unique. Just like you little girls, you are all beautiful. You are unique from each other,” said Mr. Mahinay.

questions.questionText: It seems that Jilian’s thank you card is the most beautiful. Is this a FACT or OPINION?
I think I have plenty. Is this a FACT or OPINION?
I believe your card is more beautiful than mine. Is this a FACT or OPINION?
Mr. Mahinay asked the class to group themselves and make a thank you card as an output. Is this a FACT or OPINION?
I think all of our thank you cards are beautiful. Is this a FACT or OPINION?

lessons.title: Author's Purpose
lessons.passageText:
In this lesson you will learn to identify the text’s purpose.
Text Purpose – explains why the writer presents the text in a specific way.

Read each passage carefully and identify the purpose of the text.

questions.questionText: Match the passage to its purpose:

choices.choiceText: The tarsier is a small animal found in the Philippines. It has very large eyes and can jump from tree to tree.
Last weekend, my family visited Tagaytay. We enjoyed the cool weather and ate bulalo near the lake.
How to Plant Mongo Seeds: Put soil in a small container. Place the mongo seeds in the soil. Water the seeds every day.
Children should eat breakfast before going to school. Breakfast gives energy and helps students focus during class.
One rainy afternoon, Ana heard strange noises coming from their kitchen. She saw a tiny kitten hiding under the table.
Some students leave trash under their chairs after recess. This habit makes the classroom dirty and attracts insects.

lessons.title: Making Predictions
lessons.passageText:
Making a prediction means using clues from the text to guess what will happen next.

Use the clues in the sentences to predict what will probably happen next.

questions.questionText: It was almost 4:00 in the afternoon when Bea heard the familiar bell of the taho vendor outside their house. She quickly grabbed some coins from the table and ran to the gate. What will probably happen next?

Jun and his classmates practiced every day for the Buwan ng Wika dance presentation. They memorized all the steps and wore colorful Filipino costumes during the program. What will probably happen next?

choices.choiceText: The class will perform their dance on stage.
The students will take a math test.

questions.questionText: The rain became stronger while Carlo was on his way home from school. He opened his bag and looked for the umbrella his mother told him to bring. What will probably happen next?
During lunch break, Maya opened her baon and smelled her favorite chicken adobo. Her friends looked at the food and smiled. What will probably happen next?

choices.choiceText: Maya will eat her lunch happily.
Maya will throw away her baon.
Maya will start cleaning the classroom.
Maya will go to the library.

questions.questionText: The barangay announced that there would be no classes tomorrow because of the approaching typhoon. Nico immediately checked the batteries of the flashlight and helped his mother store water. What will probably happen next?

choices.choiceText: Nico’s family will prepare for the typhoon.
Nico will go to the beach.
Nico will fly a kite outside.
Nico will play under the heavy rain.

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which word is a SYNONYM for 'large'?
An action word is called a ________.

choices.choiceText: Putting events in the order they happened

pre_post_questions.questionText: Which word is an ANTONYM for 'brave'?
A word that describes a noun is called an ________.
Why are visual elements like graphs and diagrams important?

choices.choiceText: They make the page look pretty
They give important information to help you understand
They tell you when to stop reading
